Spotlight: Off a reduced mark he won over 6f at Sha Tin last month and wasn't far away off this 5lb higher mark there next time; he's sharp enough for 5f but stall 10 asks serious questions of him.

Went up 7lb for his breakthrough win over C&D but was only half a length away upped to 6f a month ago; had no excuses that day and drawn much wider this time, while Zac Purton (aboard for last four races) has deserted him.

Without a win since January 2024 but he's not been running badly and his latest effort can be upgraded; well drawn for one who likes to be up there and Zac Purton, who has been aboard for two of his three career wins, returns for the first time in a long time.
Ran well from the front when fifth over 6f here two runs back and a slow start did for him next time; he does have competitive form off higher marks; considered.
Has only won on dirt but has threatened on turf; a greater concern would be his current form.
Down the field at Sha Tin on Saturday and that's three modest runs on the bounce; probably better on dirt.

OwnerName: Tsang Kam Chuen, Simon Siu Chak Yu Et Al
Spotlight: 13-race maiden; ran well from the front when second here in April and had wide draws when down the field on both subsequent outings; that's not the case now.
Not in good enough form to consider and that includes runs in recent trials.
Dual winner who has come a long way down the weights and has started to get competitive again; he's fine at 5f and 6f.
20-race maiden with the form to figure but he's inconsistent and was well behind Country Dancer last time.
No wins in 16 and has yet to make the frame; others have more going for them.
Won narrowly over C&D in April and beaten just over a length in a higher grade back here a month ago; has since won a trial and Britney Wong now takes off 7lb; shortlisted.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Boniface Ho Ka Kui
Spotlight: Had been running in Class 3s and appreciated the drop to this level when a front-running second over C&D two weeks ago; just 1lb higher now.
Won over 7f in New Zealand; well beaten in three Hong Kong starts and this drop in grade not enough to tempt.
Sole win came in a Class 4 over C&D in April 2025 and he's since held his own in classier company; got too far back on return to this level two weeks ago and didn't run badly; possible.

Won in New Zealand; only his second run at this level when a closing fourth over 6f two runs back and he had a wide draw next time upped to 1m; better boxed now but still has his stamina to prove over this far.
Narrow winner over C&D in April when 5lb lower; still unexposed but again poorly drawn, as last time when behind Charming Legend.
Unplaced all eight Hong Kong starts and no real evidence that he's about to put that right.

OwnerName: Chan Tung, Lo Wing Man Et Al
Spotlight: Unexposed and caught the eye when staying on into a close sixth behind in-form opposition over 7f at Sha Tin 17 days ago; looks ready for a mile.

OwnerName: Star Chan Iu Seng, Simon Chan Cham Man Et Al
Spotlight: Had lost his way but he's run well in two of his last three races, all over C&D; tried to make all last time and was third despite constant pressure; possibilities.
Won in Australia; hasn't looked at home on dirt in last two races and had been competitive before then on turf; has yet to prove himself in blinkers but no forlorn hope.
Six-race maiden with no real form claims and much to prove on several counts.
0-7 but best run yet when a close fourth here 28 days ago on first attempt at 1m; that's something to build on.
A second win over C&D when leading late on to thwart Beauty Show and others in here four weeks ago; up 6lb and drawn a little wider this time, but clearly a contender.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Pang Wai Hak, Pang Ka Yeung & Andy Pang Ka Wai
Spotlight: Won in Australia but has yet to get a lot going in Hong Kong and he'd be a surprise winner.

OwnerName: Dr V Chan Chi Tao, John Tang Lam Chek & Uri Yau Wing Hay
Spotlight: Did well in Australia but difficult to make much of a case on his local form.

All four runs over C&D under Zac Purton and going the right way after narrowly denied by Grand Nova here last month; has a 5lb pull on the winner and will exit from a similar stall; has since won a trial as well.
Has won a trial but not his latest, which came after finishing behind a handful of these here.
Some promise in his trials but trailed home last on official debut 17 days ago.

OwnerName: 24/25 Jimmy K H Ting Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: Essentially disappointing since beating Harmony Fire on his debut here last summer.
Multiple C&D winner who was bang there behind Grand Nova and Beauty Show last time; he did have no excuses though from the inside stall.
Has shown a lot more in trials than on the track, though it's still early days and he's now in a new yard.

OwnerName: Dr Philip Ho Kin Hoi, Jason Ho Chung Man Et Al
Spotlight: Made virtually all over Sha Tin's 5f in April but nowhere near that level since.

OwnerName: Billy Chan Wing Kwong, Kiki Chan Yan Ki & A Chan Fong Ki
Spotlight: Has shown enough to believe there are races in him off this mark and not a lot went right when a beaten favourite here four weeks ago, finishing behind Grand Nova and others in here; chance if this goes more to plan.

OwnerName: Daniel Chong Kwok Ming, Henry Chan Lai Yue & Ng Yim Ping
Spotlight: Shaped encouragingly on three visits to Sha Tin and then won a trial; strong in the market for Grand Nova's race here last month but early interference had him on the back foot.

RaceName: Eight To Glory, Presented By Lenovo Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Did very well to go close over 7f at Sha Tin in April, having lost significant ground at the start, and could still have untapped potential over 1m but was again slowly away when only fifth on his latest outing (7f) and is now 0-10 in Hong Kong.

RaceName: Eight To Glory, Presented By Lenovo Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Adam Chan Yam Man,Brian Wong Chi Fai & Alfred Chan Yam Lung
Spotlight: Won off a 2lb higher mark over C&D in early part of this season and was seemingly back in the groove when running-on second over C&D last month; high on the list.

RaceName: Eight To Glory, Presented By Lenovo Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Regressive 6yo who has yet to be placed after nine starts for his new stable this year; needs to turn a corner.

RaceName: Eight To Glory, Presented By Lenovo Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Alan Ho Pui Lun & Mr & Mrs Norman Tang Fai Ng
Spotlight: Came from off the pace for last-gasp C&D win in May; remains well handicapped on older form but got going too late when only sixth here last month and his chance may depend upon others setting a strong pace.

RaceName: Eight To Glory, Presented By Lenovo Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: A maiden after seven starts but beaten under 1l when placed at Sha Tin on last three appearances; nicely drawn for first run here; ought to make his presence felt again.

RaceName: Eight To Glory, Presented By Lenovo Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Close fourth over C&D in May then placed for first time in eight-race career runner-up here last month; seemingly on the verge of a breakthrough success; must be considered.

RaceName: Eight To Glory, Presented By Lenovo Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won twice over C&D in Class 5 company in April/May and ran well back at Class 4 level when fourth over 6f here five weeks ago; back up in trip today; could have a say.

RaceName: Eight To Glory, Presented By Lenovo Handicap (Class 4) (Course B) (Turf)
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden; went close over C&D at 39-1 odds in March and may not have handled the yielding ground when well beaten at Sha Tin in May but others have more convincing profiles.

Won over 7f in New Zealand; 0-7 in Hong Kong but some signs that he's dropping to a realistic mark, for all that he failed to make the anticipated progress returned to 7f (from 6f) last time; drawn wide.
0-9 in Hong Kong; appreciated drop to this grade when fifth at Sha Tin (5f) last month but this return to 6f is not sure to suit.
Now 0-14 but he's produced five competitive performances on the bounce, all here at 5f and mostly 6f; his mark hasn't moved so he's entitled to be in the thick of it once more.

OwnerName: Siu Chak Wai, Siu Wai Chung & Siu Wing Kin
Spotlight: In three of his four runs over C&D he's been beaten under 2l, last time only a length off the winner under a positive ride; still lightly raced so may do better again.
Had plenty of racing in trials but has failed to win one and he'd be a surprise winner on official debut.
Won twice here in early part of this season and back in prime form when close third under Zac Purton here last month; another turn looks near.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Stephen Ho Wai Man, F Ho Ka Chun & D Ho Ka Chung
Spotlight: Dual winner in Britain and added a 6f handicap at Sha Tin in March when only 3lb lower; has continued to run well and has form here, but his draw is a negative.
Beaten in trials; has finished closer in each of his official races, last time beaten 3.5l at Sha Tin (5f); should have more to offer but perhaps one for another day from stall 11.
Should be on a good mark and his latest close fourth here a month ago was eyecatching when he was dropped in from a wide draw; now in box 1 and can race prominently.

OwnerName: Chan Ngan Chi & Chen Yung Hung
Spotlight: All three wins have come over C&D and latest defeat was at a mile; has some solid runs to his name this year at this trip.
Some of this year's runs have been respectable but not any of his last four; bit to prove right now.

OwnerName: Rotary Club Of Kwai Chung Syndicate
Spotlight: He's proving fairly consistent at 6f without looking ahead of his mark but the handicapper is slowly relenting; should have a race in him.
Won at Musselburgh (7f, good) last May when 8lb higher but failed to hit the same heights for the remainder of last season and she's been well beaten on both outings this term.

11yo who made virtually all to win at Southwell (7f, AW) in April; unplaced on both starts since but he failed to settle and was denied a clear run here on the first occasion; has won here three times; not ruled out.

Won at Hamilton (5f, good) in May and he was back on the scoresheet at Musselburgh (7f, good) last Tuesday, making virtually all under Jack Nicholls; yard continues in flying form and he's respected under a penalty.
Won two in a row over C&D (good) in May, taking record here to 3-4; beaten favourite in AW classified since but he may well be superior on turf; high on the list returned to grass.

He's 1lb lower than when winning at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last September and is also effective on turf; however, following a break he's finished down the field on grass on his last two starts; has to get back on track.

Won four times last year; this 7yo hasn't been in the same form this season but her recent 3l fourth to Classy Clarets at Musselburgh (7f, good) was a step back in the right direction and she's a three-time winner at Catterick; remains 8lb below her last winning mark and is capable of going well.

Slow starts are becoming an issue and she's been well beaten on her last two starts; however, she showed spark when third over C&D (good) three runs ago and she won this last year (good to firm); not discounted.

Returned to form with 25-1 second of 18 at Thirsk (6f, good to firm) last week; she can't be dismissed today but she's very inconsistent and has won just one of her 21 races, while the return to 7f also poses a question.

When fifth of 14 at Beverley (7.4f, good) last month he was trapped wide and failed to settle, so that run wasn't without promise, but he's since been well beaten at Newcastle (6f, AW) and is now 0-13; cheekpieces go on; plenty to prove.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Four-time Tapeta winner, including three times this year, all off lower marks; turned in a career best on turf five weeks ago (7f, good to firm) and holds claims on that for a yard among the winners.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Second in this race last year off a 2lb lower mark; yard has been struggling for winners, while some rain would have been optimal, but she does like it here and should fare better than when favourite at Kempton early last week.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden win here (6f) on comeback in the spring, when 50-1, reads well enough; she's struggled badly in two handicaps since, however, and will need to settle better back up from sprint trips; has it all to prove now.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won a novice this time last year over (an easy) 7f round Chelmsford but has done most racing since in sprints, latterly when second in a small field here (6f) 12 days ago; will need to settle better than has often been the case.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Found a weak maiden when off the mark at long odds-on in May and is better judged on her latest handicap debut effort; cheekpieces come off for a trainer bang among the winners and this filly is one to consider under Billy Loughnane (7-40 for the yard).

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Two Polytrack wins late last summer were far better than any of her turf efforts; the handicapper's proving quick to ease off but she's hard to fancy.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Ridden more patiently than usual on her turf debut last time, back from a break, and she was inclined to pull hard (also hung left, not for the first time); that was a fair effort all things considered and she's a major player.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Plenty of racing now and she will need to improve on her two turf efforts in the spring, now back down in trip returning from a short break; others have more pressing claims.
Won from inside stall over C&D in May but was unable to land a telling blow off new mark last month; might find others better handicapped again.
Placed on consecutive C&D starts in April/May, latterly from stall 12, and was denied a clear run when only fifth here last month; remains of interest but is back in the outside box today.

OwnerName: Ng Tai Wing, Anson Or Yiu Wah & Henry Yeung Mun Kin
Spotlight: In good form in recent months, most notably when winning over C&D two starts ago; had to settle for fourth off new mark at Sha Tin 17 days ago but is well drawn again here and has each-way claims at least.

OwnerName: Ma Pak Hung & Man Pui Kwan
Spotlight: Won readily over C&D in May, his third run for Danny Shum, and ran at least as well when keeping-on third to Forerunner here last month; better drawn today and Zac Purton takes over; could still have more to offer.

Delivered on earlier promise when scoring narrowly over C&D in April, his sixth start, and quickly rebounded from a poor run when third here last month; not quite so well drawn today but not yet fully exposed; shortlisted.
Now only 1lb higher than when winning over C&D in February but has gone off the boil lately and is poorly drawn; refitted blinkers the headgear of choice today.
Didn't fire over 1m last time but went close over that trip in April and can cope with today's drop back to 6f; has form in higher-grade company this season and can't be ruled out.

OwnerName: Jason Chow Hei Shing & Chow Yuk Yin
Spotlight: Came good with C&D win in February and made the frame here on next two outings; latest run is probably best overlooked (lost action and was pulled up) but wide draw asks quite a lot of him.

OwnerName: Wilson Chu Kwok Yiu, Paul M F Cheng Et Al
Spotlight: Belatedly off the mark in Hong Kong with comfortable C&D win in April, his 22nd attempt, but was below that form on his last two appearances and his favourable draw is not enough to earn him the vote.
Unexposed after only four runs but yet to build upon his encouraging debut at Sha Tin in March; others preferred.

OwnerName: Janet Wong Hoi Shan, John Yuen Se Kit Et Al
Spotlight: Runner-up over C&D on two of his last three outings but his only win from 19 starts came in lower-grade company and he's not ideally drawn today; will probably find at least a couple in this field too strong.
Dual Class 5 winner this year, latterly when clinging on by a nose over C&D five weeks ago; looks vulnerable in this higher grade, though.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Prominent racer; she has finished second in two of her four starts, finishing clear of the third at Hamilton (5f, good to firm) 13 days ago; should give a good account now handicapping at a modest level.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Fourth in 5f novice events at Wolverhampton (AW) and Lingfield (good to firm) this summer; bred to do better and she moves into handicaps with potential; stable's record at Catterick (five wins, a second and a third from ten runners) heightens interest.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Modest form in three runs this summer, finishing behind Najmet Minzaal at Lingfield (5f, good to firm) 11 days ago; may do better now handicapping but she needs to.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Finished last on her first two starts but there was much more promise at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm; hooded first time) where she kept on for fourth having met some trouble; likely improver now handicapping but the Wetherby form isn't working out.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Improved effort when winning a Beverley claimer (5f, good to firm) in May; finished behind Mardy Bum at Wetherby last month, however, and wouldn't be open to the same progress as that rival.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Standout effort in Chester seller (6f, good) in May, finishing fourth in a useful race for the grade; failed to back it up on a slower surface at Goodwood one week later; cheekpieces added for nursery debut.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Some promise could be gleaned from his first two runs but he has been well beaten in 6f sellers on his last two starts (poor draw at Chester on first of them); drawn widest and others look safer.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Not obviously well treated on what she has achieved in her three 5f runs but she was sent off at a single-figure price for her debut and may do better now handicapping at a low level; drawn wider than ideal though.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Poor form in three novice/maiden runs; this should be more suitable but he is not easy to recommend.

Has gone backwards, latterly pulling hard when out of her depth at Royal Ascot; first two races, including her third here, might have worked out better and she has a bit to prove down to 5f for a yard that has a much better record with 2yos on the AW than turf.

Well supported at Windsor a month ago, only to blow the start, but was more professional latest, having been gelded; it's hard to know the merits of that run as yet for a trainer whose strike-rate is down with 2yos this season, but it was a decent forward step.

Trainer won with four of his last eight runners, including this filly at Nottingham last Thursday; she doesn't look particularly well in under a 6lb penalty but she may have more to offer under William Buick (2-14 for the yard).

Short price on his debut in the spring, when third of four, before again running below market expectations next time; evidently felt capable of better and is one to consider now handicapping in first-time headgear having been gelded.
Has gone backwards from her debut third on Tapeta in the spring for a yard not noted for 2yos these days; first-time headgear will need to make a difference.
Plenty went wrong on her debut and she was again inclined to edge left on soft ground last time; this half-sister to two 2yo winners should have more to offer off what looks a fair opening mark.

Did well to get as close on the AW second time, having got upset and pulled hard, but ran poorly last time; maybe that was down to the firm ground or the first-time hood (off here) but it leaves her something to prove for a yard that's 0-21 with 2yos this year.
Big prices for three runs (5f-6f) and finished well held; it is hard to make a case for her.
Made the frame over C&D on last four appearances, most recently when third from stall 1 six weeks ago; not so well drawn this time but new jockey Jerry Chau claims 2lb; probably won't be far away.
Absent since disappointing 6f run in February but was placed twice over C&D at start of this year and will be dangerous from stall 1 if fully tuned up after his break.

OwnerName: Chu Tak Wing, Casey Chu Yin Ling & Ronald Chu Tak Wah
Spotlight: Progressive 5yo who has collected three C&D wins this season and posted another career-best performance when close third here a fortnight ago; outside draw the obvious negative today, though.
Caused 21-1 surprise over C&D in April, opening his Hong Kong account at the tenth attempt, but safely held since and not easy to predict.

Dual 6f course winner in December and placed on three of his four starts this year; didn't run badly from wide stall when last in action (March) and shows enough early zip to be of interest back at 5f.
1-4 when based in Australia, the win gained in a 6f maiden; makes Hong Kong debut after ten-month absence, though, and probably best watched.

OwnerName: Dr Alan Lee Wai Kin, Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk Et Al
Spotlight: Well down the field on first three stable starts but took big step back in right direction and outran huge odds when close fifth at Sha Tin a week ago; now needs to build on that effort.
Low-mileage 5yo who won twice in Class 4 last year (5f/6f); has found things tougher since upped in grade, though, and was safely held at Sha Tin a week ago.

OwnerName: Edmund Ho Kwok Shing, Joe Fong Fu Yuen Et Al
Spotlight: Won off reduced mark in 5f Class 4 event at Sha Tin in April and put in good front-running shift when sixth (just behind Dragon Four Seas) over that C&D in this higher grade a week ago but others appeal more all the same.
Ex-Australian 4yo; launched Hong Kong career with three low-key C&D runs and has much tougher draw today; look elsewhere.
Came from off the pace to take fifth from stall 10 over C&D on stable debut a fortnight ago and has the option to revert to more positive tactics from more favourable draw today; interesting.
Scored off 2lb higher over C&D in December but recent efforts have been disappointing; needs to turn a corner.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Shaped well on soft ground on her Chepstow debut (6f) and ran to a similar level when missing out by a nose at Newbury (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; should be quick enough for this fast 5f and she holds solid claims.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: In need of the experience on her debut at Redcar (6f, good to firm) in May; more clued up when a front-running third of nine at York (5f, good) 26 days ago; one of the likelier winners.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: No match for the odds-on favourite when second over C&D (good to firm) on last month's debut but it was still a promising start to her career; open to improvement; market support would be encouraging.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £38,000 breeze-up 2yo; second foal of a maiden half-sister to Group-race winners Bocca Baciata, Topeka and Kalsa; neither of the stable's two juvenile winners this year won on debut but both ran well.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Prominent for a long way when finishing fifth of 12 at Thirsk (5f, good) in May; open to improvement but it will be needed.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Low-level form in two runs last month, the latest over C&D; minor nurseries more suitable after this.

RaceName: ATI Tank Hire Memorial Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Overcame a slow start to make a winning debut at Doncaster (7f, good; 5-2) last month by a neck from Dream Vega; wears first-time hood and steps up in trip; carries a penalty but looks to have a bright future and should go close.

RaceName: ATI Tank Hire Memorial Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Caught the eye when runner-up at this track (7f, good to soft) last month; improved when making all to beat her only two rivals at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) and looks a cracking prospect.

RaceName: ATI Tank Hire Memorial Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal; by Mind Your Biscuits; dam Japanese 6f/7f dirt winner, sister to Japanese 7.5f winner Carnet, half-sister to Japanese 1m1f winner Gran Conexion, out of Italian 1m2f Group 1 winner; makes her debut and has her work cut out against three above-average rivals.
Dual course winner this season (1m1f/1m) but not quite at best on recent outings and still on a fairly tough mark; others have more pressing claims.

Placed twice over 1m since winning over that trip here in April and this extra furlong suits him; nudged up 2lb to new career-high mark and has awkward draw to contend with but still enters calculations.
Made all over C&D in May and posted another career-best performance when close second here last month; evidently in prime form; respected.

OwnerName: Daniel Yeung Ngai & Gwenny Wu Pui Kwan
Spotlight: Gamely made all over 1m2f at Sha Tin in April, his sixth Hong Kong start, and probably bettered that performance when close second over that C&D in May; ran as though something was amiss last time and Zac Purton now jumps ship but he's a contender if back on song.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Edward Hung & Doris Hung
Spotlight: Won over C&D in early part of this season and seemed to have emerged from a dip in form when keeping-on third at Sha Tin (1m2f) last month; remains on a good mark; in the mix.

OwnerName: Peter Lau Pak Fai & Suki Tang Xianfang
Spotlight: Progressive 4yo who made all in 1m4f Group 3 handicap at Sha Tin in May and was placed in Group 3 handicap over today's trip there 17 days ago; needs to be taken very seriously.
Won 1m2f Group 2 in Australia last April but is yet to be placed after four Hong Kong starts and is seemingly in need of some help from the local handicapper.
Regressive 7yo who ought to have been suited by the return to dirt when safely held in first-time blinkers at Sha Tin 11 days ago; has usual cheekpieces back on for this turf return; revival needed.

Off the mark in Hong Kong at seventh attempt, finishing strongly from the rear for cosy C&D success last month; it's hard to predict whether this race will pan out in the same way but a recent 7lb rise looks manageable.

Close third to Le Zonda off today's mark over C&D last month and an obvious contender if judged on that performance but has very mixed record this year and needs to bounce back quickly from a poor dirt run.
Largely consistent 4yo who was placed over today's trip at Sha Tin in May; not quite in same form over 1m there last month but recent 2lb drop is helpful; considered each-way.
Dual 1m course winner this season; split Le Zonda and Glittering Legend when creditable second over C&D last month but is effectively 5lb higher from out of the weights today.

RaceName: All Races Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden; promise in maiden/novice company and fair fourth of five at Nottingham on second handicap start last time (6f, good to firm); should improve back at 7f; wouldn't rule out.

RaceName: All Races Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Placed in four of his five starts and was a creditable 2.5l seventh in a Newmarket handicap on the other occasion; disappointing third when favourite for a Windsor maiden last time (6f, good to firm; beaten more than 8l); chance back at 7f in handicap company

RaceName: All Races Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden who has a chance on his form when second at Beverley on his handicap debut (7.5f, good to soft) and when runner-up in a Thirsk maiden (7f, good) two runs ago.

RaceName: All Races Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Redcar novice last October (1m, good); rather disappointing in handicaps this season, but on a career-low mark as a result.

RaceName: All Races Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Easy winner off a 9lb lower mark on handicap debut at Ripon in May (1m, good); creditable third at Doncaster next time (1m, good to soft) but connections blamed the faster ground when well below his best last time at Pontefract (1m, good); today's sharp 7f on fast ground unlikely to suit.

RaceName: All Races Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Carlisle nursery on soft last October (6f); well below her best all three runs since; return to faster ground a concern.

RaceName: All Races Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Carlisle nursery last August (6f, good); best run since when fourth of 20 at York last time (7f, good; third and fifth have won since) when Harswell Angel was eighth; chance.

RaceName: All Races Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Didn't get the best of runs when eighth of 20 at York two runs ago (7f, good; Dacres Cross fourth); fair fifth at Doncaster last time (7f, good); each-way chance.

RaceName: All Races Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed maiden; some promise in maiden/novice company (including over C&D); rather disappointing when well beaten on handicap debut at Doncaster last time (7f, good to firm); bit to prove.

RaceName: Great Yarmouth Racecourse Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 200,000gns yearling whose opening Salisbury fourth (1m2f, good) in May came behind three talented rivals; he ran well below market expectations, though, in finishing a long way off the front three and will need to settle better if he's to progress.

RaceName: Great Yarmouth Racecourse Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: In at the deep end when sixth on his debut last September, in a 1m Newbury conditions event, and it spoke in his favour that he started among the favourites; it will be interesting to see how this 300,00gns foal goes in the market this time after ten months off.

RaceName: Great Yarmouth Racecourse Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: P Hickman And Lord Lloyd Webber
Spotlight: Well-related 360,000gns yearling who was beaten a long way on his soft-ground debut a month ago; bettered that at Redcar next time but handicaps may be his time to shine after today.

RaceName: Great Yarmouth Racecourse Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Inclined to pull hard and cracked late on over 1m4f at Royal Ascot, having bumped into a few progressive winners previously; sets the standard back into maiden company but can hang right and has had eight cracks at it now.

RaceName: Great Yarmouth Racecourse Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Second on debut last autumn; comeback run on Tapeta was too bad to be true and she got back on track upped to this trip (good to firm) last time when beaten a neck at Doncaster; the sex allowance brings her into the equation for an in-form yard, under William Buick (10-43 for them).

RaceName: Great Yarmouth Racecourse Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 400,000gns yearling who made a fair debut at Nottingham recently (1m2f, good) when chasing home a long odds-on favourite; entitled to have learned from that.

Progressive for new stable this season, winning twice (once over C&D) and making the frame six further times; gave another good account when fifth from stall 12 here last month and is much better draw today; ought to be right in the thick of things.
Four wins this season, the latest over in April; posted sound effort when fifth over C&D last month but will need to find something extra to overcome this career-high mark.
Came good in Hong Kong at fifth attempt, with C&D win in April, but has been safely held twice since (albeit from wide draws) and needs to kick on again.
Took form to new level when scoring over C&D last month and is now 2-8 in Hong Kong; latest 8lb rise asks quite a lot of him but he still commands respect.

Has twice made all in Class 4 this season (once over C&D) but was reeled in fairly comfortably when back up in this grade last month and, although much better drawn this time, may remain vulnerable at this level.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Simon Ng Kar Ming
Spotlight: Just held on from King Profit to exploit much-reduced mark over C&D last month and remains well handicapped on older form but he's getting long in the tooth and others have more appealing profiles.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs William Y W Lai, Stanley Y C Hong & Sammy Y C Long
Spotlight: Steadily progressive 5yo who won readily over 5f here in May and lost out only by a short head to Power Koepp over C&D last month; Zac Purton takes the ride today; likely contender.

OwnerName: Arthur Antonio Da Silva, Betty Da Silva & Teresa Da Silva
Spotlight: Won consecutive C&D races in March/April and rebounded from a duff effort when second over C&D a fortnight ago; deserted by Zac Purton today and not ideally drawn but still enters calculations.
Made respectable stable debut over 6f at Sha Tin in May but finished last over 5f here a few days later and looks opposable from stall 11.
Kept on for fourth over C&D on last month's stable debut and remains on a workable mark but double-digit draw dents his appeal; usual cheekpieces left off.
Posted sound effort when fifth over C&D in April and has another good draw today but this is his first run for 11 weeks ago and he has not been firing on all cylinders this season.
Dual 5f winner in Australia; has shown clear promise over that trip in Hong Kong but is yet to be placed after four local runs and has tough draw for this first attempt at 6f.

RaceName: Bet At racingtv.com Handicap (For The Turmeric Challenge Trophy)
Spotlight: Won over hurdles at Hereford in May and followed up on the Flat over this C&D (good to firm); it's disconcerting he was pulled up on his chase debut at Stratford three weeks ago but he may well bounce back and play a leading role reverting to this code.

RaceName: Bet At racingtv.com Handicap (For The Turmeric Challenge Trophy)
Spotlight: Runner-up on the Flat last September and he's been in top form over hurdles of late at Market Rasen, winning two in a row (2m/2m4f, good) before finishing second on his last start; respected back in this code.

RaceName: Bet At racingtv.com Handicap (For The Turmeric Challenge Trophy)
Spotlight: Completed 1m6f AW hat-trick early this year but he's been a long way below that level on turf on his last three starts and is 0-15 on grass.

RaceName: Bet At racingtv.com Handicap (For The Turmeric Challenge Trophy)
Spotlight: His losing sequence continues but he's dropped down the weights and his third of five at Beverley (1m4f, good to firm) last Friday in a first-time visor (retained) was slightly more encouraging; could build on that.

RaceName: Bet At racingtv.com Handicap (For The Turmeric Challenge Trophy)
Spotlight: Very modest form over hurdles and well beaten at 25-1 on her Flat handicap debut here (1m4f, good) two months ago; she's hard to fancy on the back of that but the first-time tongue-tie may help this bumper winner and she's not written off.

RaceName: Bet At racingtv.com Handicap (For The Turmeric Challenge Trophy)
Spotlight: Course winner who scored at Hamilton last June; however, she's failed to get competitive on her five starts since last September (two of them this year) and needs to find something extra.

Highly competitive with James Owen for last four starts, notably when running away with a Brighton handicap (7f, good to firm) eight days ago; creditable second there yesterday with a hood added; had 1m form last season; highly respected if she's brought out quickly again.
0-7 and he has beaten just three rivals home in his four handicap starts; tumbling down the weights but carries risk.
Five-time AW winner but still looking for a first turf win; well beaten in a C&D handicap six weeks ago; on a winning mark but not exactly solid.
Came from an unpromising position to win a 7f handicap at Doncaster (good to soft) in May; has not matched it in two runs since and this isn't sure to set up for him.
Three times a course winner, including over 1m last July; ran into an improver here four weeks ago and she looks set to give another good account.
Poor strike-rate and his three runs for his new trainer haven't suggested he was primed to double his tally.
Ended a losing run when winning a 1m handicap at Ripon last month; fair fourth at Carlisle (7f, good to firm) two weeks ago; should give her running but one or more of the 3yos may have her measure.

In good form on AW over the winter, winning at Wolverhampton (6f) and Lingfield (7f); 1lb lower than for the latter win but she hasn't maintained her form back on turf this summer, including behind Velvet Rhythm over C&D 16 days ago.

She made a promising debut at Carlisle (6f, good to firm) last summer and then scrambled home in a 6f novice at Southwell in October; disappointed at Southwell on her seasonal/handicap debut 12 weeks ago (trainer's representative reported that she failed to stay 7f on her first run after a six-month break); possible there will be stronger stayers in today's field but she's unexposed and wears a first-time tongue-tie.

OwnerName: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
Spotlight: Improved for handicapping this year, always prominent when winning at Newmarket and over C&D in May and then overcoming a slow start back here 16 days ago; another 4lb rise to contend with but she should make a bold bid to maintain her unbeaten course record.
Two encouraging runs over 6f here for her new stable; steps back up to 7f for her handicap debut; not fully exposed just yet.

Made all for an easy win at Redcar (7f, good) three weeks ago; had to work harder to follow up at Musselburgh (1m, good) three days later; drop back to 7f can help but she is another 6lb higher in her hat-trick bid and this is a rise in class.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Hasn't counted in three maidens, showing mild promise; upped in trip now.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Three maiden runs all on soft/heavy, taking a backward step at Limerick last time; tongue-tie fitted and contrasting ground conditions.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Ballinrobe maiden at just shy of this trip was okay but stamina test (2m1f) may have been too much at Limerick latest; back in trip with visor now replacing cheekpieces, can go well.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Beaten a long way in all five starts, last twice in handicaps; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Shaped creditably on handicap debut last month over just shy of this trip at Gowran, staying on third after a slow start; big chance with a repeat of that run.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Ballinrobe claiming third probably her best effort; safely held in handicaps since; seemingly tough task at the weights.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Beaten a long way in four maidens over shorter trips; blinkers tried latest now replaced by tongue-tie and cheekpieces and steps up in trip.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Has had plenty of chances, pick being Gowran handicap third (1m), lost action at Ballinrobe latest; weighted to go well.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Fourth in similar Ballinrobe contest recently; basement 40 mark leaves her with lots to find.
Ex-Ed Dunlop maiden; two fair runs over hurdles and not a bad effort on the AW for her current yard last summer, and the market should tell a tale back from the best part of a year off.
Longstanding maiden who's proved very expensive to follow in AW handicaps over the past year or so; didn't improve on earlier turf efforts at Leicester last month but this race is weaker again.

Won a 0-50 classified event over C&D last summer and followed up in the same race 12 days ago; not always consistent and is no certainty to back it up, going without cheekpieces this time, but holds major claims under Billy Loughnane.

OwnerName: M L W Bell Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Tapeta winner early in the year who's achieved little in a handful of runs on turf; has been as far as 1m6f and drops from 1m4f, returning from a few months off.

Bumped into a better-than-class winner in a soft-ground 0-50 (first-time visor) over C&D last month; easy to back under different conditions at Brighton next time, when well held over 1m4f having pulled hard; hard to know what to expect in truth.
Longstanding maiden who's regressed; tongue-tie quickly removed and she's not obviously fancied back from a couple of months off.
Six-time AW winner who has more off days than on now; better off with Zafaan than when fifth behind him over C&D 12 days ago, back into a handicap today, but needs more.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Soldier's Call filly; dam unraced half-sister to Italian 5f winner Isolina, out of maiden sister to 6f 2yo/2m hurdle winner Freedom Square, is granddaughter of sister to 1m Listed winner Speirbhean (dam of Dewhurst winner Teofilo); likely best watched on debut.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Won a barrier trial and ran a cracker in a maiden at Cork (6f, good) when beaten 0.5l into second by subsequent Group 3 winner Ballinea Star; the one to beat if building on that excellent effort.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Sent off at 12-1 on debut over C&D in median sires series race a week ago and caught the eye finishing a 3l second after meeting trouble in running; enters calculations now with improvement likely to be forthcoming.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Huge odds and no impression in 6f maidens at the Curragh and Cork; others preferred.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith
Spotlight: Siyouni filly; dam 1m winner (inc 2yo/Group 3; RPR 104), half-sister to winners Propose (1m2f) and Minerva (7f 2yo), out of US 6.5f/1m Grade 2 winning sister to 1m Group 1 winner Mohaather; Lordan prefers stablemate but still worth a market check.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith
Spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly; half-sister to four winners inc Armory (7f-1m2f inc 2yo Group 2; RPR 119), Subsequent (11.6f-14.5f inc Listed; 111) and HMS Seahorse (1m4f Flat, 100; useful 2m/2m4f hurdle); dam Group-placed 6f 2yo winner (108); looks top yard's first string on rider arrangements.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Promise in both starts; beaten 2.5l into third of six on debut by subsequent Queen Mary winner Victorious at Naas (6f, good) in April; not as good next time upped to 7f at the Curragh when beaten 8.25l; drop back in trip here a plus and has a chance on that debut effort.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 33-1 and well held on debut at Cork in the race in which Argentarius finished second; much more needed.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Saxon Warrior filly; dam Listed-placed 7f winner (RPR 99), half-sister to winners Bella Isabella (Group-placed 7f) and Sara Valentina (6.6f), out of 1m1f Group 3 winning half-sister to useful 7f winners Joailliere and True Solitaire; market best guide on debut.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: State Of Rest filly; E85,000 yearling; half-sister to winners Yaahobby (5f-7f AW; RPR 70) and Aussie Nation (Australian 1m2f/10.5f); dam 6f/7f 2yo winner (inc Group 3; 104), closely related to useful 6f-1m winner Band Width; in good hands; check the market.

RaceName: Industry Leading In-Play Markets At Unibet Apprentice Handicap (Training Series) (Qualifier)
Spotlight: On a losing run but she got right back on track with third of eight in 11.5f handicap at Lingfield in May; not out of things off the same mark.

RaceName: Industry Leading In-Play Markets At Unibet Apprentice Handicap (Training Series) (Qualifier)
Spotlight: Gained a second 1m win here in May but only eighth of 11 in 1m Newmarket handicap 18 days ago; goes well round here but has his stamina to prove on his first try beyond 1m.

RaceName: Industry Leading In-Play Markets At Unibet Apprentice Handicap (Training Series) (Qualifier)
Spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who is much improved for the switch to Richard Hughes, impressively landing 1m4f handicaps at Brighton and Epsom recently; races off a 10lb higher mark here (including 5lb penalty) but hard to oppose.

RaceName: Industry Leading In-Play Markets At Unibet Apprentice Handicap (Training Series) (Qualifier)
Spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with C&D success and has continued in good form, back from two months off with second of 14 over C&D four weeks ago; must enter calculations nudged up 1lb.

RaceName: Industry Leading In-Play Markets At Unibet Apprentice Handicap (Training Series) (Qualifier)
Spotlight: A three-time winner (including over C&D) in 2025; yet to hit top form this season though, slowly away when sixth of eight in 1m2f Pontefract handicap last month; needs to take a step forward.

RaceName: Industry Leading In-Play Markets At Unibet Apprentice Handicap (Training Series) (Qualifier)
Spotlight: Sole success came over 1m4f here (for Barry Brennan) in March 2024; in decent nick for his current yard, staying on into fourth of nine at Newbury (1m3f) 15 days ago so not ruled out back up in trip.

RaceName: Industry Leading In-Play Markets At Unibet Apprentice Handicap (Training Series) (Qualifier)
Spotlight: Resumed winning ways over 1m4f here in May and backed it up with a solid third of nine in 1m4f Leicester handicap 25 days ago; considered.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: No signs yet of winning potential in maidens over C&D (good) and at Roscommon (7.5f, good); may need more time and experience.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Blackbeard colt one of two for top yard; the choice of Moore when 100-30 on debut at the Curragh last month but beat just one rival home; will need to improve plenty on that and looks the stable's second string on rider arrangements here.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt; E400,000 yearling; dam French 1m/1m1f winner (inc Listed; RPR 106), out of maiden sister to 1m3f Listed winner Parthenon, half-sister to Group 3 winners Magna Graecia (15.5f) and Graikos (1m1f); preferred by Lordan and should go well on debut for top yard.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
OwnerName: Philip Antonacci & AC Crawford Farms LLC
Spotlight: Kingman colt; 160,000 yearling; closely related to French 1m4f winner Senguun; dam unraced half-sister to French 1m2f winner Frankish, out of 1,000 Guineas winner Miss France; showed ability when second in Naas barrier trial; interesting.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Mehmas colt sent off at 12-1 on debut at the Curragh (6f, gd-yld) and wasn't knocked about when keeping on well from the back in the closing stages for an 8.5l sixth of 13 after a sluggish start; likely to know more now and could go well in first-time blinkers.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Ten Sovereigns colt; dam 1m AW 2yo/Qatari 1m2f winner (RPR 86), closely related to Scandinavian 1m 2yo-1m7f winner Zakopane, half-sister to winners Admiral De Vega (Swedish 1m1f Group 3) and Zeeyara (French 1m2f Listed) behind King's Fury when seventh in Naas barrier trial; probably best watched.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: No Nay Never colt; 80,000gns yearling; half-brother to 5f 2yo winner Dinamo (RPR 52); dam 1m AW winner (70), half-sister to winners Go Angellica (1m 2yo Listed) and Southern Belle (useful 5f/6f inc 2yo); one of two for yard and market can guide on debut.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Starman colt; 400,000gns yearling; half-brother to Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner Letherfly (RPR 93); dam 5f AW winner (83), half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Orlaith, out of unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 1 winner Toormore; likely type on paper from respected source, with some big-race entries; can be a player.

OwnerName: C Munro, Mrs M Gander And Partner
Spotlight: She has made steady improvement in her three races and stepping back up in trip looks the right way to go considering how she stayed on to take fourth at Leicester (5f, good to firm) on her most recent outing in May.
Her best run so far was when third in a maiden over C&D (good to soft) in May; needs a personal best to figure here.
Ran two solid races on soft over 5f last month; needs to show she can act on faster ground on this step up in trip.
Has shown ability on all three starts, latest when third of nine in a Leicester maiden (7f, good) last month; drops back in trip and should run well.
Has shown just modest form so far and needs to show significant improvement on this step up in trip.
Looks nothing special based on his three runs so far; has been gelded since his last run in April; blinkers are now introduced on this step up in trip and they need to have a positive effect.
Well held in her three runs so far and she needs to take a major step forward on this step up in trip with a first-time tongue-tie applied.

RaceName: Unibet/British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Kodiac filly who built on debut second with fourth of 11 in 6f Windsor novice nine days ago; she may do better still so is not out of things.

RaceName: Unibet/British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Good Effort filly; took a step forward from her debut when sixth of nine in 7f Newmarket novice 19 days ago, fading in the final furlong; she can build on it back at 6f now.

RaceName: Unibet/British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 75,000euros foal, 55,000gns yearling, 115,000gns breeze-up 2yo; dam, runner-up over 6f (RPR 68), is a half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Kilmah; this Blue Point filly is in very good hands and must enter calculations.

RaceName: Unibet/British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Is being brought along steadily, a patiently-ridden seventh of ten in 6f Newbury novice 15 days ago; capable of better in due course.

RaceName: Unibet/British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 16-1, seventh of eight in novice at Nottingham (6f, good) on her debut in April; lots more is needed after a break.

RaceName: Unibet/British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fair form when runner-up over C&D and in 6f Hamilton maiden this summer; holds very good claims.

RaceName: Unibet/British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 16-1, very slowly away when last of 11 in maiden at Pontefract (6f, good) on her debut 30 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Unibet/British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Duaij Al Khalifa & Ahmad Al Qassimi
Spotlight: £100,000 yearling; dam, 1m winner (RPR 93), is a half-sister to winners On The Balcony (1m1f) and Spiritofhayton (Italian 2m/2m2f hurdle), out of unraced half-sister to Group-placed 6f 2yo winner Remember You; yard took this 12 months ago; interesting newcomer.

RaceName: Unibet/British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 10-1, shaped encouragingly when a debut fifth of ten in maiden at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) 20 days ago; this Sioux Nation filly fetched 475,000 guineas as a yearling and seems sure to do better.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Talented dual-purpose performer is only 1-8 on the Flat but brought her mark to a career-high at Ballinrobe last month when just denied close home in fillies' handicap over 1m5f; quick ground a slight concern, big player if handling it.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Two bumper wins and was gaining a second win on the Flat when readily taking 1m6f Gowran handicap in April (soft); 8lb higher and quicker ground to contend with (has won on good).

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
OwnerName: E Lynch & T C Quinn & John J McGrath
Spotlight: Smart staying hurdler/chaser, midfield in BetMGM Handicap at Cheltenham in March when last seen; lightly raced on Flat, winning 1m5f Ballinrobe maiden last season, well held in Leopardstown handicap subsequently; may just need this comeback run with Galway on the horizon.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Talented hurdler whose three Flat wins include 1m4f Tramore handicap last summer; not seen since midfield in 2m Naas premier handicap in October; another likely Galway-bound, jockey booking encouraging.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: In fine form over 1m4f on the AW since return from a break, winning off 72 in early April and close second off revised mark later that month; didn't count on turf return at Gowran last month and hood now added to usual tongue-tie.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Capable under both codes, both Flat wins coming in 1m6f Killarney handicaps, last summer off 72 before good second at Newmarket (2m2f); Curragh comeback run in late May was poor but more expected of here.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Four Flat wins at up to 1m4f, including Leopardstown handicap last season off 78; yet to hit form this campaign though, twice not appearing to stay today's trip; bit to prove now.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Sole win came on AW (1m4f maiden); well beaten both turf outings this term and looks to have a fair bit to find.

RaceName: bet365 GBB Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Band D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed improved form stepped up in trip when third in a Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) 13 days ago; if he can replicate that form back on turf then he must hold strong claims.

RaceName: bet365 GBB Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Band D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beat only one home on his debut in a Windsor maiden (5f, good to firm) last month and needs to show much more on this step up in trip.

RaceName: bet365 GBB Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Band D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Gelded prior to showing improved form when fourth on his second start at Bath (5.5f, good) last month; steps up in trip and should run well.

RaceName: bet365 GBB Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Band D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed just modest form in a couple of Salisbury maidens over 6f last month; steps up in trip and more is required.

RaceName: bet365 GBB Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Band D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £15,000 foal; tenth foal; by Walzertakt; half-sister to winners Adagio (1m4f Flat, RPR 81; smart 2m hurdle), Adaliz (2m4f/3m hurdle), Against All Odds (German 1m/9.5f) and Ad Acta (French 2m1f/2m2f hurdle); dam German 1m winner; likely to need the run on debut and should be better suited by middle distances.

RaceName: bet365 GBB Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Band D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Improved on her debut run when stepped up in trip to finish fifth of 11 at Kempton (7f, AW) nine days ago; going the right way.

RaceName: bet365 GBB Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Band D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ran a couple of solid races last month over 6f at Newbury and at this track; needs to show more though on this step up in trip.

Sea The Stars filly who looked a good prospect when making a winning start in 9.5f Wolverhampton novice in November; off since but she remains with lots of potential (has Group 2 entry at Glorious Goodwood) and looks the one to beat despite conceding weight all round.
Has offered little in a Worcester bumper and 9.5f maiden at Wolverhampton this summer; significantly more is required.
Stimulation filly; fair form at best shown in her three bumpers in 2025; blinkers go on for her belated Flat debut though after six months off the track.
A fair hurdles winner for Claire Harris in 2023 but this veteran mare offered little over hurdles and fences for Kevin Pickard last season; Flat debutante.
25-1, hinted at promise with fourth of five in maiden at Catterick (1m4f, good) on her debut in April; can take a step forward.

OwnerName: Hot To Trot Racing1 & Mrs Fiona Read
Spotlight: Nathaniel filly; sister to Polish 1m2f winner Stavraetos and closely related to 1m3f/2m AW winner Caprelo (RPR 85); also a half-sister to numerous other winners, notably Marmelo (1m4f-1m7f Group 2; RPR 117); dam maiden (RPR 64); yard has enjoyed plenty of success with this family so she's a considered newcomer, especially if the market vibes are positive.
18-1, hinted at promise when fifth of eight in maiden at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) on her debut 20 days ago; this daughter of Masar can build on it over this longer trip.
Cracksman filly who came in 11th of 14 on her debut in 7f AW Lingfield novice in November; she's open to progress over this much longer trip though.
15-8, shaped well when a debut third of 13 in 1m2f AW maiden at Lingfield last month; enters calculations with better to come.
40-1 and very green when ninth of ten in maiden at Goodwood (1m2f, good to firm) on her debut 19 days ago; this Cracksman filly should be all the better for the experience.
Sent off 22-1 but made a promising start with fourth of 14 in maiden at Newbury (1m2f, good) in April; this daughter of Lope De Vega is a likely player with more to offer.
33-1 and hooded when a well-held seventh of eight in C&D maiden on her debut four weeks ago; significantly more is needed.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Progressive last season, two wins included Listed contest at Leopardstown (1m6f, good) and signed off with very creditable fifth in the Group 2 Park Hill at Doncaster; has to bounce back from a poor comeback run in Gowran Listed in early May and cheekpieces tried.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Unlimited
Spotlight: Dual-purpose mare, whose only Flat win came in Leopardstown 3yo maiden (1m2f); in good form however this term in very competitive handicaps, second at Navan, close fourth of 15 in 2m2f Chester Cup and 4.5l off stablemate Kizlyar when seventh of 20 in 2m4f Ascot Stakes last month; bit to find with some of these now entering Group company but don't rule out.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Last year's winner who went on to post a career-best when second of 24 in Group 1 Melbourne Cup (2m, good-to-soft); last month's Limerick comeback run well below her best but entitled to come on for that and major chance on ratings.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Maiden twice runner-up in good-ground maidens last year and career-best when beaten 5.5l in 1m6f Gowran Listed in May; never sighted in Group 3 Munster Oaks at Cork on latest; again has plenty on her plate here.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Maiden has been highly tried at times to no avail but did pick up some black type when third behind Floresta in Leopardstown Listed last term; not a bad effort when fourth in Gowran Listed in May but this is a stronger race.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Winner of three of her six races for Ralph Beckett; purchased for 300,000gns and cracking effort on seasonal return/stable debut when just denied in 1m2f Curragh premier handicap in May; didn't land a blow but not beaten far in Group 3 Munster Oaks latest (first-time cheekpieces) so not one to dismiss lightly.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: Cork 2yo maiden winner highly-tried in three runs this term when tried in pacemaking role, notably third behind Thundering On in 1m2f Navan Group 3; blinkers had no effect when down the field in Munster Oaks latest and now replaced by cheekpieces and upped in trip.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Mrs H Shanahan & Mrs Jane Shanahan
Spotlight: Ran away with 1m4f Fairyhouse maiden (good) last month but limitations seemingly exposed 18 days later in Roscommon Listed over same trip; upped in distance here.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Group-placed 2yo winner last year who has finished last in all three runs this term, most recently Group 3 Munster Oaks (two of today's rivals ahead).
Landed a maiden over C&D last June when trained by Jack Channon; she has been well below that form for current yard this year and the step back up in trip needs to spark significant improvement.

Had shown hints of promise on first three starts but was last of 11 on handicap debut at Salisbury (6f, soft) last month in first-time blinkers (removed here); steps up in trip and has questions to answer.

Both wins have came on AW over 6f at Wolverhampton; raced too freely when a beaten favourite here (6f, good to firm) 11 days ago; steps back up in trip and needs to settle better to give herself a chance of getting home.

Got off the mark at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in May off 5lb lower; ran well at Catterick and back at Wolverhampton subsequently but found his latest race at Bath (1m, good) when 12th of 14 too competitive; this looks easier and he holds claims.

Showed little in three AW novices last back-end but much improved when making a winning turf/handicap debut at Leicester (7f, good to firm) in May; she should be able to cope with a 5lb rise and looks the one to beat.

Opened his account at Lingfield (6f, good; 33-1) in May in first-time cheekpieces which are back on here after a visor last time didn't have any impact; needs to get back to his best on this step up in trip.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Progressed well in 2025 when a dual C&D winner; shaped encouragingly after six months off when fourth of five in 2m Goodwood handicap on his return in May; sort to do well again this season, so he's a big player eased 1lb.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Baileys Horse Feeds (F H Nash Ltd)
Spotlight: Gained a deserved first success of the season in three-runner handicap at Newbury (2m) 15 days ago; up 3lb but he remains quite low mileage so needs considering.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with C&D win in January but his more recent form is far less encouraging, last of nine at Chester (2m) last time; others are preferred.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Completed a 2m AW hat-trick over the winter and she posted another good effort with second of five in 1m4f handicap at Epsom in April; that form has been well advertised since so she's not taken lightly eased 1lb after a break.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Gained a first success at Southwell (1m3f) in November and has added two more AW victories since, including over 1m3f here; not disgraced with fifth of 15 at Newbury (1m4f) last time and well in the mix if his stamina holds out on his first run beyond 1m4f.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Resumed with C&D success and backed it up with a solid fourth of 13 over C&D later in May; ought to be in the shake-up off the same mark.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Winless on the Flat since 2024 but she arrives in good form from a spell hurdling (scored at Wincanton in April); not out of things back in this sphere.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: A dual 1m4f scorer who signed off for Alan King with a solid fourth of ten in 1m4f handicap at Wolverhampton in March; has since changed hands for 10,000gns and needs to hit the ground running for his new yard.

RaceName: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Fair staying maiden; in decent nick over hurdles when last seen for Denis Hogan in May so she's no forlorn hope starting out for her new yard here.
Neither run devoid of promise, over 5f at Dundalk and when keeping on over 6f at the Curragh last time; should appreciate the step up to 7f; might outrun likely odds.
80-1 and soundly beaten over 1m4f here on debut; can only watched down markedly in distance.

OwnerName: One Eight Seven Four Syndicate/Paul J Cahill
Spotlight: Modest efforts all three starts over 5f-6f on yielding ground and on the AW; will probably find his level when tackling handicaps.

Narrowly denied on first two starts in 7f maidens at Naas (yld-sft) and Gowran (yld); not disgraced when pitched into Group 1 company at the Curragh last time (1m, good); rated 102 and the one to beat back in these calmer waters.
150-1 and beaten 22l on debut at Naas (1m, good); seems safe to rule out on the back of that tame effort.

Dark Angel gelding; E220,000 yearling; brother to 7f winner Dark Tornado (RPR 99), half-brother to three winners inc Raintree (7f; 77) and Marie Paradis (1m2f/1m4f; 59); dam 9.4f AW winner (69); bred to be decent but beat just one home in Naas barrier trial.
Triple figure odds for both starts and ran accordingly; look elsewhere.
Very modest efforts at big odds in 1m maidens at Gowran and Leopardstown; unlikely winner.

Well-bred Into Mischief colt ran a pleasing race over 6f on debut at Dundalk in January when sent off at 16-5; absence since a bit of a concern and drawn wide but pedigree suggests this trip should suit and worth monitoring in the market.
Soundly beaten in maidens at Dundalk last November and on turf at Limerick in June; will qualify for handicaps after this.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: In the first three in three of his four starts (all on easy ground) and rated 83; attracted the attention of the stewards when eased up late for third at Gowran (7f, soft) last time in first-time cheekpieces (discarded); should be in the mix again if handling these quicker conditions (dam related to good ground winners).

River Boyne gelding; second foal; brother to 5f winner Boyne Lady (RPR 74); dam twice-raced (60) half-sister to winners Centennial (1m4f Group 2) and Siren's Song (1m2f Listed), out of half-sister to 1m1f/1m2f Group 1 winner Croco Rouge; worth a market check on debut.
Down the field at big odds in maidens over C&D and at Limerick on good and soft ground; may need more time.

Rated 74; standout effort in three previous maiden runs a 0.75l third of 9 at Leopardstown (7f, good); disappointing run off 77 on handicap debut at the Curragh (1m, good), weakening into last of 12; dropping back to 7f is a plus but drawn wide and probably place claims at best.

Rated 72; fine effort on debut in June of last year at Leopardstown (7.5f, good) but nowhere near that level in three subsequent runs; off since a moderate run on testing ground over 6f at the Curragh in March; quicker ground and step back up in trip here a plus but more needed.

RaceName: Each Way Extra At bet365 Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Landed her first win on turf at Bath (1m, firm) in May on her stable debut; ran well in two runs back there since but looks vulnerable off 3lb higher.

RaceName: Each Way Extra At bet365 Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Returned to winning ways when scoring over C&D (good) last month off 4lb lower; raced too freely at Ffos Las two days later and may be worth considering back at his last winning venue.

RaceName: Each Way Extra At bet365 Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Beaten double-digit lengths in three maidens; steps up in trip on handicap debut and may well show improvement.

RaceName: Each Way Extra At bet365 Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 11th attempt at Brighton (1m, good to firm) eight days ago; needs to contend with a 6lb penalty but she may have more in her locker and is in with a big shout.

RaceName: Each Way Extra At bet365 Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Reliable sort with five wins in the bag; a beaten favourite on his last two starts, his turn may be near once more but he is 2lb higher than his last winning mark.

RaceName: Each Way Extra At bet365 Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has seven wins and usually at his peak in the summer months but below his best at Bath on his last two starts; others preferred.

RaceName: Each Way Extra At bet365 Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Landed a nursery for Joseph O'Brien last October in first-time blinkers (absent here); gelded since and makes British/stable debut for yard that knows the time of day; raced on no firmer than good but may be way ahead of the handicapper; market will reveal more.

RaceName: Each Way Extra At bet365 Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Wildly inconsistent maiden who showed little when last of six at Brighton (1m2f, good to firm) on stable debut last month; difficult to fancy.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Ran his best race when fifth of 13 in a Windsor novice (1m2f, good to firm) last month; the drop back to 1m on his handicap debut may not be in his favour though.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Scored on his second start for Hugo Palmer at Newcastle (7f, AW) in December; ran well to be placed in handicaps at Lingfield AW and Wetherby recently; has since changed hands and sports first-time cheekpieces replacing blinkers on stable debut; should not be far away.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Third of ten in a newcomers race at Kempton (7f, AW) last August, but two backward steps in the autumn; latest was on heavy going, so might be excused and he needs a second look on this handicap/seasonal debut.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Scored on his nursery debut at Leicester (1m, good) last October; gelded subsequently and pulled too hard on his resumption when ninth of 14 at Bath (1m, good) last month; may do better if he settles.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Best run when third of seven on her return to action in a Kempton maiden (7f, AW) in April; beaten 3l at Carlisle (1m, soft) on handicap debut in first-time blinkers (swapped for first-time visor here) last month and she may have more to give.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 0-8 and has shown bits of form, but appeared to resent not being able to lead when trailing in last at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last month.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Yet to get off the mark but has been placed twice in handicaps this year; first-time cheekpieces are applied and he should be suited by this step up in trip.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Placed in a couple of handicaps at Yarmouth on first two starts this season (1m/1m2f, good/good to firm); lost all chance when badly hampered when favourite at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) last month; compensation may be waiting.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Placed twice in five completions last year, but having been gelded he has run below that form in a couple of handicaps this term; first-time blinkers worn last time are persevered with.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Best form shown was on second start last year when fourth in a Wolverhampton maiden; ran well below that form subsequently and has it all to prove on reappearance/handicap debut.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Best effort was when beaten a neck on her second start in a Southwell novice (7f) last October; shaped a bit better when fifth of nine at Carlisle (1m, soft) in first-time cheekpieces (retained) last month and Oisin Murphy rides for the first time.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Placed twice in handicaps here in April/May but below that level twice since; needs the return to this track to bring about a resurgence.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Opened her account at Ffos Las (1m, good) last month when making all; up 4lb and she needs to show she is as effective on AW.

RaceName: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Placed three times in a row before getting off the mark in a ten-runner handicap at Lingfield (1m, Polytrack) on Monday, doing it in very smooth style at 8-11; no shock if he follows up under a 6lb penalty.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
OwnerName: Richard McNally & Leo Walsh & L Murrin
Spotlight: Four-time winner, including two premier handicaps; below best on first two runs in Listed company this term and failed to beat a rival back in handicap company latest; is now 1lb below his last win but a revival is needed for last year's winning stable.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
Spotlight: A winner in France for Andre Fabre; not bad efforts the last twice, including over C&D on penultimate; eased 1lb for a 5.75l ninth of 17 at the Curragh 12 days ago when racing a touch keenly after a slowish start; not discounted.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
OwnerName: W Jones/D W Sloan/M C Mocchetti/Kabin Racing Syndicate
Spotlight: 2-7 on the AW but is 1-13 on turf (win came on yielding); won at Dundalk in March off 3lb higher; the best of three turf runs since then came over 7f at Cork on penultimate; not as good dropped back to 6f at the Curragh on latest; moves back up in trip now and might prefer an ease in the ground.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
OwnerName: Thomas J O'Connor & Ms Hazel McGuinness
Spotlight: Eight-time winner on the AW but just 1-31 on turf; beaten 3l when third of 6 in this last season and just 1lb lower now; finished a couple of places ahead of Tolebi when a 4.5l seventh of 17 at the Curragh on latest; place shout.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
Spotlight: AW maiden winner has yet to get her head in front on turf (0-10) but has run some fine races in defeat; not a bad effort when she had two of these behind at the Curragh latest (7f, good); eased 2lb and can go close if the new tongue tie ekes out a bit more.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
Spotlight: Generally consistent filly; won for a second time at Leopardstown in April (7f, yld-sft); almost added to her tally over 6f (good) here on latest when denied by a head; the step back up to 7f is fine and ground versatile so she has to enter calculations off just 2lb higher.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time winner has been running well this season but yet to get his head in front; all his victories have come over 6f at this venue; 3lb wrong here but he ran well in this race last season to finish a 2.5l second off 1lb higher; thereabouts.

Five of his six wins have come on AW; didn't look straightforward without usual hood or tongue-tie (back on here) when fourth of seven at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) 12 days ago; two things in his favour here are that he is the only distance winner in the field and this represents a drop in grade, so he needs to be considered seriously.
Placed on plenty of occasions here and last month's overdue first win was also registered at this track (6f, soft); 1-32 win record can't be forgotten but he holds place prospects.
1-26 but generally consistent; didn't show any obvious improvement in first-time cheekpieces (retained) when third here (6f, good to firm) 11 days ago and likely to be competing for minor honours.
12-race maiden making his seasonal/stable debut following nine-month absence; down to a career-low mark but probably best watched for now.
Has struggled since landing two wins in the summer of 2024; visor returns replacing the first-time blinkers worn last time.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Lingfield (1m, AW) in June but scuppered his chance (not for first time) with a very slow start when beating one at Wolverhampton (8.5f) since.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Scored twice (over 1m1f) at Goodwood last summer; yet to hit top form this season though, a never-dangerous fifth of eight back at Goodwood (1m1f) last month; has slipped to a lenient mark though so needs considering in a headgear changed for in-form yard.

RaceName: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Arrives in very good form without winning, a clear second of 11 in 1m Bath handicap 25 days ago; drawn wide but this course winner merits serious consideration with Oisin Murphy back up.
